reasoning: The question tasks us with forecasting whether the Moody's AAA Corporate Bond Yield relative to the 10-Year Treasury Yield (AAA10Y spread) will be higher on a future resolution date than it was on 2025-11-09. With the most recent known value (2025-10-30) at 1.050 (105 basis points), the spread is relatively tight compared to historical averages, which slightly increases the likelihood of a widening (increase) in the medium-term due to mean reversion. However, financial and economic indicators typically follow near-random-walk patterns over short intervals (such as ~3 months until the question close date). The empirical base rate for FRED questions is given as 0.422, indicating a historical leaning against "increases" for this baseline. Balancing the tightness of the spread (favoring an increase) with the empirical base rate (favoring a decrease/staying flatter), an estimate close to an even probability but slightly leaning towards the base rate is appropriate.
